content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 14, 1990

Dan Weinstein
Los Angeles County Federation
of Labor
2130 West 9th Street
Los Angeles, CA 90006

RE: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-90-588

Dear Mr. Weinstein:

This is to confirm telephone advice I gave to you on
September 12, 1990 concerning the duties of the Sheetmetal
Workers' Union under the contribution limits of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You have asked whether the following activities are
permissible under the Act: The Sheetmetal Workers' Union (the
"union") desires to raise money for contributions to California
candidates. May the union solicit contributions from individual
delegates at its international conference in Washington, D.C.,
deposit the monies into its political action committee's account
and then make contributions from that account to its various
California and non-California union affiliates for possible
contribution to California candidates?

___________________________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-90-588
Page 2


As I indicated to you on the telephone, these activities will
not violate the contribution limits or other provisions of the Act
if the following conditions are met:

1.      When the union contributes the funds to its California
or non-California affiliates it cannot earmark the funds
for contribution to particular California candidates.
If the funds are earmarked for this purpose, then they
will be considered a contribution directly from the
union to the California candidate. In such a case the
funds will be cumulated with all other funds similarly
earmarked and the total of such funds cannot exceed the
contribution limit for California candidates applicable
to the union.2

2.      The same person or persons cannot in fact direct and
control the making of contributions to California
candidates in excess of the applicable contribution
limits. (See Regulation 18531.5, copy enclosed.3) In
other words, if the same person or persons make
contribution decisions for the union and any of its
affiliates, the contributions from the union and those
affiliates will be considered to be from the same source
and, when cumulated, cannot exceed the contribution
limits applicable to that source.

3.      The amounts given to the affiliates by the union for
contribution to California candidates cannot exceed the
applicable limits on contributions to committees under
the Act.4




2 The contribution limit for California candidates that is
applicable to the union will depend on the type of committee it is
as defined by the Act. If the union is not currently a California
recipient committee, the enclosed information sheet will assist in
making this determination.

3 Even though Regulation 18531.5 has not yet been filed with the
Secretary of State, it has been approved by the Commission and is
an expression of Commission policy. The regulation is currently
under review by the Office of Administrative Law and should be
filed with the Secretary of State within the next month.

4 The limits applicable to the union's contributions to each
affiliate will be either $2,500 (see Section 85302(a)) or $5,000
(see Section 85302(b)), depending on the affiliate's status. (See
enclosed information sheet and Campaign Information Manual.)

Our File No. A-90-588
Page 3


4.     The contributions made by the affiliates cannot exceed
the applicable limits on contributions to candidates.5

     Finally, please note that the giving of these funds may, at
specific points, require the filing of appropriate campaign
reporting forms.  Please refer to the enclosed Campaign
Information Manual for detailed information concerning campaign
disclosure.

     If you have any questions concerning this matter.  Please
contact our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division at
(916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

Enclosures




_____________________
5 The contribution limits applicable to each affiliate will depend
on its status.  (See enclosed information sheet and the enclosed
Campaign Information Manual.)
